FixpointResolveDriverInput.docs: Input for `sotp track fixpoint-resolve` at the cli_driver boundary (IN-12). Carries stdlib-typed fields only (mirrors the driver-boundary DTO convention already used by DryWriteDriverInput etc.); track_id / current_branch are re-validated into domain::TrackId / FixpointCurrentBranch directly inside FixpointResolveDriverInteractor (usecase, which already has domain access) as the first step of its own orchestration (ADR 1328 D4) -- not by an infrastructure adapter.

FixpointResolveDriverOutcome.docs: Structured (pre-render) output for `sotp track fixpoint-resolve` at the cli_driver boundary (IN-12/AC-17). Mirrors domain::track_phase::FixpointStep's four variants as a usecase-level DTO -- cli_driver may only depend on usecase (architecture-rules.json), so the domain enum itself cannot cross this boundary -- plus a Failure variant for validation/port/interactor-level errors, constructed only by FixpointResolveDriverInteractor (which performs the fixpoint-resolve orchestration directly per ADR 1328 D4, not via a delegate infrastructure adapter -- see FixpointResolveDriverInteractor's docs). `scopes` mirrors domain::track_phase::ReviewScopeSet's non-empty scope-label set flattened to Vec<String> (individual scope labels are untyped String even in the domain ReviewScopeSet). Rendering this into CommandOutcome text (the former format_fixpoint_step) now happens in cli_driver.

FixpointResolveDriverInteractor.docs: Implements FixpointResolveDriverService by orchestrating the fixpoint-resolve invocation directly in usecase (ADR 1328 D4): validates track_id (domain::TrackId::try_new) and current_branch (FixpointCurrentBranch::try_new) and enforces the TrackNotActive branch-mismatch guard as pure logic, then resolves workspace paths and the track's configured base_branch via FixpointWorkspaceContextPort, loads the dry-check capability config via DryCheckConfigLoaderPort, obtains a request-scoped FixpointDryGateService via FixpointDryGateFactoryPort and invokes its resolve_dry_gate to obtain the current dry-approval service and diff fragment refs, obtains request-scoped ReviewGateStatePort/RefVerifyGateStatePort instances via FixpointGateStateFactoryPort, constructs usecase::fixpoint_resolve::FixpointResolveInteractor from these resolved values and invokes FixpointResolveService::resolve directly -- no infrastructure SecondaryAdapter sits between this interactor and the domain-level resolve call -- then maps the resulting domain::track_phase::FixpointStep into FixpointResolveDriverOutcome (converting ReviewScopeSet into Vec<String> for RunRfp). Any validation, port, or interactor failure is converted to FixpointResolveDriverOutcome::Failure { message }. Mirrors FixpointDryGateInteractor's established shape (multiple fine-grained secondary ports injected, orchestration performed by the usecase interactor itself) rather than DryDriverInteractor's single-port pass-through shape: an earlier draft of this entry held a single coarse `port: Arc<dyn FixpointResolveDriverPort>` field and pushed the FixpointResolveService::resolve invocation and its surrounding orchestration into an infrastructure SecondaryAdapter, which was flagged as an ADR 1328 D4 violation (2026-07-02 plan-artifacts final-review P1 finding) and corrected here; FixpointResolveDriverPort no longer exists.

FixpointWorkspaceContext.docs: Output of FixpointWorkspaceContextPort::resolve_context: the validated workspace paths (CWD-anchored discovered repo root, canonicalized/containment-checked items_dir, derived project root) and the active track's configured base_branch (read from metadata.json's branch_strategy_snapshot.base_branch; resolve_context returns Err(FixpointWorkspaceContextError::Unavailable) instead of a hardcoded branch-name default when metadata.json is missing or fails to decode -- fail-closed per CN-03/CN-06, no branch-name fallback), bundled together because base_branch resolution is structurally dependent on the validated canonical_items_dir (mirrors DryCorpusMetaPort::resolve_corpus_meta's precedent of returning multiple related resolved values from one cohesive port method). usecase-hosted Dto (role R1 note: usecase Dto placement needs justification) justified because this is the internal output shape of a usecase SecondaryPort method, directly analogous to the existing FixpointResolveDriverInput/FixpointResolveDriverOutcome usecase-hosted plain data carriers in this same module -- it never crosses a serde boundary. `base_branch: String` (R9/R10 note): the base-branch concept already has a proper domain-level home (domain::branch_strategy::BranchStrategySnapshot, wrapping a validated domain::NonEmptyString, introduced by this same track's T001/T002), so R10's "model the concept somewhere in domain" requirement is satisfied elsewhere; this field is a resolved, already-domain-validated runtime value threaded through to FixpointDryGateFactoryPort / FixpointGateStateFactoryPort for `git rev-parse <base_branch>` / adapter-construction use, matching the raw String/&str representation already used at this exact orchestration boundary by every sibling entry introduced by IN-02/IN-04/IN-11 (BranchStrategyPort::base_branch, FsDiffBaseResolverAdapter::new(base_branch: String), FsReviewGateStateAdapter::new(items_dir, base_branch: String)) -- re-wrapping it in a second, usecase-local newtype here would diverge from that established convention without adding a new invariant.

FixpointWorkspaceContextError.docs: Error returned by FixpointWorkspaceContextPort::resolve_context when repo discovery fails, items_dir cannot be canonicalized or escapes the repository root, items_dir is not a directory, or the track's metadata.json cannot be read/decoded. Single opaque-message variant, mirroring the existing DiffBaseResolverError / DryCorpusMetaError shape in this same crate.

DryCheckConfigLoaderError.docs: Error returned by DryCheckConfigLoaderPort::load when `.harness/config/dry-check.json` cannot be read or parsed. Single opaque-message variant, mirroring the existing DiffBaseResolverError / DryCorpusMetaError shape in this same crate.

DryWriteOutcome.docs: Structured (pre-render) output for `sotp dry write` at the cli_driver boundary (IN-13/AC-18). Replaces the previous DryDriverOutcome-based pre-formatted stdout: DryDriverPort::dry_write / DryDriverService::dry_write now return this type so the CLI text rendering (the former dry_write_outcome helper) moves to cli_driver. `Success` carries plain counts and per-finding summaries; `Failure` carries an adapter-level error message, mirroring the existing DryDriverOutcome::failure convention -- DryCompositionRoot::dry_write itself always produces `Success` inside its own `Result::Ok`, `Failure` is constructed only by the DryDriverPort adapter when the composition call returns `Err`.

DryWriteFindingSummary.docs: One rendered-finding row carried by DryWriteOutcome::Success (IN-13). Fields mirror domain::dry_check::DryCheckFinding's changed_fragment_ref / candidate_fragment_ref / refactor_proposal, flattened to String because cli_driver may only depend on usecase (architecture-rules.json) and these values are opaque display text at this boundary, already validated upstream in the domain layer -- the same raw-String-for-domain-concept convention already used by DryWriteDriverInput.track_id at this same driver boundary.

DryCheckApprovedOutcome.docs: Structured (pre-render) output for `sotp dry check-approved` at the cli_driver boundary (IN-13/AC-18). Mirrors domain::dry_check::DryCheckApprovalVerdict's Approved/Blocked shape as a usecase-level DTO (cli_driver may only depend on usecase, so the domain enum itself cannot cross this boundary) plus a Failure variant for adapter-level errors, matching the DryDriverOutcome::failure convention. DryCompositionRoot::dry_check_approved itself only ever produces Approved/Blocked; Failure is constructed only by the DryDriverPort adapter.

DryDriverInteractor.docs: Baseline pass-through interactor implementing DryDriverService by delegating to an injected DryDriverPort. Referenced (not modified) so that its existing impl of DryDriverService -- whose dry_write / dry_check_approved signatures this track changes (IN-13) -- is accounted for by the catalogue. DryDriverInteractor's own struct shape and constructor are unchanged; only the delegation methods' return types shift to match the modified trait.

methods.name: base_branch
methods.docs: Returns the base branch name (branch from which track branches are created).

methods.name: merge_target
methods.docs: Returns the merge target branch name (branch into which track branches are merged).

methods.name: merge_method
methods.docs: Returns the merge method (Squash, Merge, or Rebase).

methods.name: track_prefix
methods.docs: Returns the track branch prefix. Always returns 'track/' per CN-04.

BranchStrategyPort.docs: Port for reading the effective branch strategy. Implemented by JsonConfigBranchStrategyAdapter (reads from config file) and SnapshotBranchStrategyAdapter (reads from metadata snapshot).

methods.name: switch_base
methods.docs: Switch to the base branch read from the active track's branch_strategy_snapshot. The default implementation returns a failure placeholder until composition-root wiring overrides it.

methods.name: review_status
methods.docs: Query the current review gate status for track_id.

ReviewGateStatePort.docs: Baseline port referenced so that the FsReviewGateStateAdapter -.impl.-> ReviewGateStatePort edge is visible in the contract-map (R7). The port's own method signature is unchanged by this track; FsReviewGateStateAdapter's added base_branch field (IN-04/AC-04) only affects the adapter's internal fallback logic.

methods.name: resolve_diff_base
methods.docs: Resolve the diff-base commit for the given track directory.

DiffBaseResolverPort.docs: Baseline port referenced so that the FsDiffBaseResolverAdapter -.impl.-> DiffBaseResolverPort edge is visible in the contract-map (R7).

methods.name: build_approval
methods.docs: Build a DryCheckApprovalService for the given track.

DryApprovalFactoryPort.docs: Baseline port referenced so that the FsDryApprovalFactoryAdapter -.impl.-> DryApprovalFactoryPort edge is visible in the contract-map (R7) after the adapter's relocation from cli_composition to infrastructure (IN-11). The port's own method signature is unchanged by this track.

methods.name: resolve_context
methods.docs: Resolve the validated workspace paths (CWD-anchored discovered repo root, canonicalized/containment-checked items_dir, derived project root) and the given track's configured base_branch for this invocation.

FixpointWorkspaceContextPort.docs: Secondary port for the fixpoint-resolve orchestration (IN-12). Bundles CWD-anchored git repo discovery, items_dir canonicalization/containment validation, project-root derivation, and base_branch resolution from the track's metadata.json branch_strategy_snapshot behind one cohesive port method, mirroring DryCorpusMetaPort's precedent of resolving several related filesystem-dependent values in one method. Fail-closed per CN-03/CN-06: resolve_context returns Err(FixpointWorkspaceContextError::Unavailable) -- never a hardcoded branch-name default -- when metadata.json is missing, unreadable, or fails to decode; no branch-name fallback. Implemented by infrastructure::track::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FsFixpointWorkspaceContextAdapter (ADR 1328 D7: port-impl adapters belong in libs/infrastructure). Injected directly into FixpointResolveDriverInteractor, which invokes it as part of its own orchestration (ADR 1328 D4) -- unlike the superseded FixpointResolveDriverPort design, this port performs only a single narrow I/O concern and does not itself invoke FixpointResolveService.

methods.name: load
methods.docs: Load the usecase dry-check capability configuration and its content fingerprint from `.harness/config/dry-check.json` under repo_root.

DryCheckConfigLoaderPort.docs: Secondary port for the fixpoint-resolve orchestration (IN-12). Replaces the inline `InfraDryCheckConfig::load(...)` + `build_usecase_dry_check_config_pub(...)` call previously embedded directly in the (now-removed) `TrackCompositionRoot::fixpoint_resolve`, keeping infrastructure config-DTO access out of the usecase interactor. Implemented by infrastructure::track::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FsDryCheckConfigLoaderAdapter. Injected directly into FixpointResolveDriverInteractor, which uses the returned config both to build the FixpointDryGateCommand (via FixpointDryGateFactoryPort's constructed service) and to construct usecase::fixpoint_resolve::FixpointResolveInteractor.

methods.name: build
methods.docs: Construct a FixpointDryGateService bound to the given base_branch. Infallible: every constituent adapter this factory wires is either stateless or takes only base_branch as its runtime value.

FixpointDryGateFactoryPort.docs: Secondary port for the fixpoint-resolve orchestration (IN-12). usecase::fixpoint_resolve::FixpointDryGateService's only implementation (FixpointDryGateInteractor) needs a base_branch-bound usecase::fixpoint_resolve::DiffBaseResolverPort instance (infrastructure::dry_check::diff_base_resolver::FsDiffBaseResolverAdapter::new(base_branch)) at construction time, and base_branch is resolved at request time (per-track, from metadata.json), so FixpointDryGateInteractor cannot be a permanently-injected field of FixpointResolveDriverInteractor -- it must be built fresh per invocation via a factory, mirroring the existing DryApprovalFactoryPort pattern. Implemented by infrastructure::track::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FsFixpointDryGateFactoryAdapter, which reproduces the wiring previously done by the (now-removed) `TrackCompositionRoot::make_dry_gate_interactor` helper (NoOpDryApprovalService + FsDiffBaseResolverAdapter + FsDryCorpusMetaAdapter + DryFragmentPipelineInteractor + FsDryApprovalFactoryAdapter, all still infrastructure-internal). Injected directly into FixpointResolveDriverInteractor, which invokes the returned service's resolve_dry_gate directly (ADR 1328 D4: FixpointResolveDriverInteractor -- not an infrastructure SecondaryAdapter -- owns this step of the orchestration).

methods.name: build_review_gate
methods.docs: Construct a ReviewGateStatePort bound to the given items_dir and base_branch. Infallible: pure object construction.

methods.name: build_ref_verify_gate
methods.docs: Construct a RefVerifyGateStatePort bound to the given items_dir. Infallible: pure object construction.

FixpointGateStateFactoryPort.docs: Secondary port for the fixpoint-resolve orchestration (IN-12). usecase::fixpoint_resolve::ReviewGateStatePort / RefVerifyGateStatePort's only implementations (infrastructure::track::gate_state::FsReviewGateStateAdapter / FsRefVerifyGateStateAdapter) take items_dir (and, for the review gate, base_branch) at construction time; both are request-time values, so neither adapter can be a permanently-injected field of FixpointResolveDriverInteractor -- both must be built fresh per invocation via a factory, mirroring the existing DryApprovalFactoryPort pattern. Two independent methods (rather than one bundled method) because RefVerifyGateStatePort's construction genuinely does not need base_branch. Implemented by infrastructure::track::fixpoint_resolve_driver::FsFixpointGateStateFactoryAdapter. Injected directly into FixpointResolveDriverInteractor, which passes both constructed ports directly into usecase::fixpoint_resolve::FixpointResolveInteractor::new (ADR 1328 D4: FixpointResolveDriverInteractor -- not an infrastructure SecondaryAdapter -- owns this step of the orchestration).

methods.name: ref_verify_status
methods.docs: Query the current ref-verify gate status for track_id.

RefVerifyGateStatePort.docs: Baseline port referenced for contract-map edge visibility: FixpointGateStateFactoryPort.build_ref_verify_gate returns Arc<dyn RefVerifyGateStatePort>. The port's own method signature is unchanged by this track.

methods.name: resolve_dry_gate
methods.docs: Resolve the dry gate for the given command.

FixpointDryGateService.docs: Baseline application service referenced for contract-map edge visibility: FixpointDryGateFactoryPort.build returns Arc<dyn FixpointDryGateService>, and FixpointResolveDriverInteractor invokes resolve_dry_gate directly on the returned value. The service's own method signature is unchanged by this track.

methods.name: fixpoint_resolve
methods.docs: Resolve the next fixpoint step for the given input.

FixpointResolveDriverService.docs: Application service (primary port) for `sotp track fixpoint-resolve`, invoked by cli_driver::track::TrackDriver (IN-12/AC-17). TrackDriver holds this service, calls it (invoke), and renders the result into CommandOutcome; TrackCompositionRoot no longer calls the FixpointResolveService interactor directly from the CLI dispatch path. Implemented by FixpointResolveDriverInteractor, which performs the fixpoint-resolve orchestration itself (ADR 1328 D4) rather than delegating it to an infrastructure adapter -- see FixpointResolveDriverInteractor's docs for the full orchestration shape. This trait's own single-method signature is unchanged from the superseded design; only its implementing Interactor's internal shape changed.

methods.name: dry_write
methods.docs: Run `sotp dry write`.

methods.name: dry_results
methods.docs: Run `sotp dry results`.

methods.name: dry_check_approved
methods.docs: Run `sotp dry check-approved`.

methods.name: dry_fix_local
methods.docs: Run `sotp dry fix-local`.

DryDriverPort.docs: Secondary port for the `dry` command family, implemented by an adapter in cli_composition that delegates to DryCompositionRoot / DryFixRunnerCompositionRoot methods. IN-13: dry_write / dry_check_approved now return structured pre-render DTOs (DryWriteOutcome / DryCheckApprovedOutcome) instead of the pre-formatted DryDriverOutcome, so the CLI text formatting moves to cli_driver; dry_results / dry_fix_local are unchanged.

methods.name: dry_write
methods.docs: Run `sotp dry write`.

methods.name: dry_results
methods.docs: Run `sotp dry results`.

methods.name: dry_check_approved
methods.docs: Run `sotp dry check-approved`.

methods.name: dry_fix_local
methods.docs: Run `sotp dry fix-local`.

DryDriverService.docs: Application service trait for the `dry` command family, invoked by cli_driver::dry::DryDriver. IN-13: dry_write / dry_check_approved now return structured pre-render DTOs (DryWriteOutcome / DryCheckApprovedOutcome); DryDriver renders these into CommandOutcome text itself instead of forwarding an already-formatted string.

methods.name: new
methods.docs: Create a new interactor bound to the four fine-grained secondary ports it orchestrates directly.
